ops.title: Skill(codex) from a schema-bound workflow wrapper returns a bare \"Skill execution completed\" stub — run codex exec via CLI in wrappers instead
ops.body:
Invoking `Skill(codex)` from a workflow `agent(..., {schema})` wrapper silently loses the answer: the caller's `StructuredOutput` tool leaks into the skill's `context: fork`, the fork ends its turn on it, and the relay harvests only trailing text — so the wrapper sees the byte-identical stub `Skill "codex" completed (forked execution). Result: Skill execution completed` and fabricates an empty result (e.g. `findings: []`).

**Why:** Diagnosed 2026-07-07 in cc-context (15/18 reproductions) — see `~/.claude/plans/two-durable-lessons-indexed-sun.md`; hit again 2026-07-08 in this repo's gitobj-fix review gate, where a "clean" finder pass had never run codex at all.

**How to apply:** Wrappers must run the CLI directly: write the self-contained question to a `mktemp` path, `cat q | codex exec -c model_reasoning_effort=xhigh -c service_tier=fast -o <reply> --sandbox workspace-write` (retry once with `--skip-git-repo-check` on "Not inside a trusted directory"), read the reply, return it verbatim. Never trust an empty review without fresh reply-file evidence. Main-session `Skill(codex)` is unaffected (no schema tool to leak). Once the `codex:codex-wrapper` agent type ships in cc-skills (plugin v0.9.0 plan), spawn that instead.
